description: Dau's example of complex ligature handling in Peirce's Beta graphs, demonstrating how lines of identity can cross cut boundaries to represent quantifier scope.
natural_language: There exists something that is P but not Q.
test_rationale: This example tests the system's ability to handle ligatures that cross cut boundaries, which is a key feature of Peirce's Beta graphs. It demonstrates how quantifier scope is represented through the placement of lines of identity relative to cuts.
